<tool_call>
<function=Edit>
<parameter=file_path>
src/uniq.js
</parameter>
<parameter=content>
/**
 * Remove duplicate values while preserving first-occurrence order.
 */
function uniq(items) {
  return items.filter((item, index) => items.indexOf(item) === index);
}

module.exports = { uniq };
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

src/palindrome.js
</parameter>
<parameter=content>
/**
 * Return true if s reads the same forwards and backwards.
 *
 * Should be case-insensitive and ignore whitespace.
 */
function isPalindrome(s) {
  const cleaned = s.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, "");
  return cleaned === [...cleaned].reverse().join("");
}

module.exports = { isPalindrome };
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

agent_raw_output:
All three tests now pass. Here's a summary of the bugs I fixed:

1. **`src/chunk.js`** - The original implementation used `Math.floor(items.length / n)` to calculate the chunk size, which caused items to be dropped when the array length didn't divide evenly by `n`. Fixed by iterating through the items with a step of `n` instead.

2. **`src/uniq.js`** - The original implementation used `Set` which doesn't preserve order, and then sorted the result. Fixed by using `filter` with `indexOf` to keep only the first occurrence of each item, preserving the original order.

3. **`src/palindrome.js`** - The original implementation didn't handle case-insensitivity or spaces. Fixed by converting to lowercase and removing all whitespace before comparing.

shadow.reason: All 6 tests now pass, confirming all three bugs were fixed and verified.
shadow.summary: Fixed chunk.js (iterates by step size n instead of k), uniq.js (uses filter with indexOf instead of Set.sort), and palindrome.js (adds toLowerCase and whitespace removal). All tests in test/*.test.js now pass. Task complete.
